flashcards.definition: The apparent shift of a nearby star against distant stars as Earth orbits — used to find distance.
Distance at which a star has a parallax of 1 arcsecond; ≈ 3.26 light-years.
Mean Earth–Sun distance ≈ 1.496 × 10⁸ km; used as the baseline for parallax.
The angle between two objects as seen from an observer.
Historically calculated using triangulation from two points on Earth's surface.
